statement.p: Simplify this expression, and if applicable, write the restricted domain.

solution.p: To simply the complex fraction \frac{{\frac{1}{x-4}+\frac{5}{x-4}}}{{2-\frac{1}{x+4}}}, we multiple the numerator and denominator with the common denominator of {\frac{1}{x-4}+\frac{5}{x-4}} and {2-\frac{1}{x+4}}, which is {\left(x+4\right)\mathopen{}\left(x-4\right)}.
Since x+4 was canceled, x \ne -4 is a lost domain condition.
Although x-4 was also canceled, x \ne 4 is not a lost domain condition because it is implied in the solution.
